reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 170 cfs @ Fort Collins , 7 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir It is runoff time on the Poudre, the bigger water comes with big bugs! We are starting to see salmonflies come off in the lower Poudre. The colder weather this week will delay the hatch, but still keep an eye out for those salmonflies. The colder weather should also bring some more stable flows this week. Don't be hesitant to fish your biggest foam flies this time of year. Larger stonefly and caddis droppers have been finding success on dry dropper rigs. The streamer game has been productive as well sticking with darker olive and black to find some willing fish. We are getting more guide trips out on the Poudre every week. Give us a call to get the latest river report and current conditions! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year. Or, click the images below for a full list of our current recommended fly patterns.
